items.blurb: Fifty-four scraps of old-world charm by Lin Chia Ning, ready to make your journal look like a treasured attic find.
Brooch-style nature stickers with glinting gold foil — like pinning a tiny botanical garden onto your planner pages.
Gold-kissed floral drops that turn any journal spread into a little bouquet. Handmade-brooch energy, zero sewing required.
Cafe-core in sticker form: foil-trimmed lattes and beans for anyone whose planner runs on caffeine too.
Everyday objects, elevated — these foil-accented drop stickers make the mundane look museum-worthy in your journal.
Dessert-cart stickers with a gold foil shimmer. All the pastry-shop delight, none of the crumbs on your planner.
A snoozing kiwi bird to nap on your to-do list — die-cut sticky notes so soft-looking your reminders feel tucked in.
A dozing shiba curled on your desk in sticky-note form, plus a dreamy companion sheet for longer messages.
A cat asleep on the job, as nature intended — these die-cut sticky notes make forgetting things almost worth it.
A sleepy pup for your paperwork: a small dog-shaped note for reminders and a dreamier one for proper messages.
A polar bear mid-hibernation on your memos — the coolest, calmest way to leave yourself a note.
Another napping kitty for your desk rotation — die-cut sticky notes that treat every reminder like a bedtime story.
The internet's favourite fluffball bird, fast asleep on a sticky note. Reminders rarely come this round and cosy.
A panda snoring gently across your desk notes — proof that even your to-do list deserves a soft landing.
An ultra-fine 0.38mm gel pen wearing Heisuke Kitazawa art — the cushioned tip moves with you, so notes glide as smoothly as it looks.
A weighty vintage-body gel pen dressed in Heisuke Kitazawa's art — the kind of pen that makes writing a grocery list feel ceremonial.
A cookie-shaped Hattifatteners clip that holds your papers together while looking entirely snackable. Moomin Valley, but make it desk organisation.
A cookie-shaped Snufkin clip that holds your papers together while looking entirely snackable. Moomin Valley, but make it desk organisation.
A cookie-shaped Little My clip that holds your papers together while looking entirely snackable. Moomin Valley, but make it desk organisation.
A cookie-shaped Moomin clip that holds your papers together while looking entirely snackable. Moomin Valley, but make it desk organisation.
Puffy Little My stickers with all her signature attitude in squishy 3D — Moomin Valley's fiercest tiny resident, now on your journal.
Eighty square sheets of Moomin across four designs — your reminders were never going to settle for a plain yellow pad again.
A cat hugging its plush best friend, carved into a stamp — one press adds instant softness to any card or journal page.
Two bears in matching outfits holding up your gratitude for you. Thank-you notes basically write themselves now.
Party-hat cats clutching hearts — stamp it on the good days and watch your diary cheer along with you.
A rabbit fairy floating in on a balloon, ready to sprinkle a little wonder over your letters and lists.
A pocket-sized notebook of dapper little animals in outfits, with paper made to take stamps beautifully. Joy collection starts here.
A tiny illustrated signboard stamp by Midori Asano — perfect for flagging the important bits of your planner with quiet charm.
Midori Asano's answer to scheduling: a sweet little stamp that asks the time so your planner doesn't have to nag.
One small press, one soft meow. Midori Asano's cat stamp gives every page a feline sign-off.
A friendly hand-drawn hello by Midori Asano, ready to open every letter and journal entry on a warm note.
Subikiawa's laid-back little scene in stamp form — press it wherever your week needs permission to slow down.
A bird that thinks it's a plane, by Subikiawa. Stamp it on anything that needs a dose of deadpan whimsy.
A serene floating penguin from Subikiawa — the patron saint of doing absolutely nothing, now available for your planner.
A busy little penguin hauling its cargo, Subikiawa-style. For the days your to-do list needs a mascot.
Vegetables, animals and delightfully random household objects parade across this pocket notebook — Subikiawa's pop world in A6.
Shuku Nishi's tree stamp brings a still, storybook forest to your pages — one press and the woods move in.
A quietly noble squirrel by Shuku Nishi, rendered like a woodblock print. Autumn forest vibes for your planner, year-round.
A girl balancing an apple on her head, drawn by Shuku Nishi — equal parts fairytale and quiet mischief in stamp form.
Shuku Nishi's swan glides on like shadow art — an elegant silhouette stamp for letters that deserve a little grace.
Swans drifting through a starlit forest on the cover, stamp-friendly paper inside — Shuku Nishi's dreamiest little notebook.
A pencil-behind-the-ear bear giving an emphatic GOOD! — the stamp of approval your journal (and your friends) deserve.
A round little seal asking, in Swedish, if you'd like a tea break. The politest fika invitation your planner will ever get.
A small dog with a small bag on a big adventure — Mariko Fukuoka's gentle nudge to step outside, in stamp form.
A bear lost deep in a book, stamped mid-ponder. For the readers, the thinkers, and the professionally distracted.
Tiny motifs scattered across an A6 cover, stamp-ready paper within — Mariko Fukuoka's pocket companion for travel stamps and stray thoughts.
Robin-chan the black cat, fast asleep in a rose teacup — a stamp so cosy it might tuck your planner in at night.
Robin-chan with a head-tilt and something clearly very important to say. Let her deliver your margin notes with maximum poise.
Robin-chan dressed up with her little bag, off on an outing — stamp her wherever your week has plans.
Robin-chan caught mid-roll in a perfect loaf of momentum. The stamp equivalent of a cat video, for your notebook.
Robin-chan and her dip pen grace this A6 notebook — part stamp album, part ticket keeper, entirely a black-cat lover's dream.
Mariko Fukuoka's dessert doodles on soft Mino washi — one-stroke writing paper sized exactly for a sweet little message.
Washi note slips illustrated by Mariko Fukuoka, made for the notes that only need one warm line and a lot of heart.
A bakery's worth of illustrated loaves on velvety washi slips — leave someone a note that smells (almost) of fresh bread.
Tadashi Nishiwaki's gentle dog-and-human moments on washi note paper — for messages as warm as a lap-nap.
C is for cat, and for cute correspondence — Reiko Tada's feline washi slips make every quick note purr.
A lineup of certified good dogs on fine washi paper, waiting to hand-deliver your one-line notes with tails wagging.
Cats, lemon sweets and 100 sheets of washi that folds into origami — Shoichiro Tobimatsu makes note-taking foldably cute.
One hundred washi sheets of cats and macarons that moonlight as origami paper — notes now come with a folding hobby attached.
Tobimatsu's cats and confections on a washi memo pad that doubles as origami stock — ten designs, endless tiny cranes.
Foil-stamped washi stickers of lemon confections by Shoichiro Tobimatsu — patisserie-counter prettiness for your planner pages.
Foil-stamped washi stickers of macarons by Shoichiro Tobimatsu — patisserie-counter prettiness for your planner pages.
Foil-stamped washi stickers of assorted pastries by Shoichiro Tobimatsu — patisserie-counter prettiness for your planner pages.
A hundred sheets of cats politely posing beside lemon sweets — Shoichiro Tobimatsu's memo pad turns errands into little dessert dates.
A hundred sheets of cats politely posing beside macarons — Shoichiro Tobimatsu's memo pad turns errands into little dessert dates.
A hundred sheets of cats politely posing beside sweets — Shoichiro Tobimatsu's memo pad turns errands into little dessert dates.
Fourteen flake stickers of cats and lemon-sweet treats in two handy sizes — Tobimatsu's dessert shop, scattered across your journal.
Fourteen flake stickers of cats and macaron treats in two handy sizes — Tobimatsu's dessert shop, scattered across your journal.
Double sticky-note sheets pairing Tobimatsu's cats with lemon-sweet treats — reminders have never looked this dessert-forward.
Double sticky-note sheets pairing Tobimatsu's cats with macaron treats — reminders have never looked this dessert-forward.
Double sticky-note sheets pairing Tobimatsu's cats with strawberry treats — reminders have never looked this dessert-forward.
A letter set on delicate rakusui washi, decorated with Tobimatsu's lemon sweets — the kind of paper that makes people keep your letters.
A letter set on delicate rakusui washi, decorated with Tobimatsu's macarons — the kind of paper that makes people keep your letters.
A letter set on delicate rakusui washi, decorated with Tobimatsu's strawberry treats — the kind of paper that makes people keep your letters.
A Rei Kurahashi greeting card with a hidden second illustration inside — a small surprise waiting the moment they open it.
A whisper-thin roll of clear tape covered in pop-art cats — five metres of feline sparkle for planner margins everywhere.
Rainbow cats on a slim clear tape running five metres long — enough technicolour kitties to decorate a year of journal spreads.
Kitchen tools drawn in soft coloured pencil across 120 memo sheets — Kano Hirabayashi makes even the whisk look huggable.
Everyday Japanese dishes rendered in gentle coloured pencil — a 120-sheet memo pad that makes your notes look delicious.
An entire bustling town from a bird's-eye view, sliced into an A5 sticker sheet — build Narumi Suzuki's tiny city across your journal.
